context: Tests whether the agent identifies both violations: entity importing TypeORM decorators (outer-layer framework in inner layer) and use case instantiating a concrete repository (bypassing interface contract).

checklist.name: TypeORM decorator violation identified
checklist.description: VIOLATIONS.md identifies that Order entity importing TypeORM decorators violates the dependency rule (inner layer importing from framework/outer layer)

checklist.name: Concrete repository instantiation violation identified
checklist.description: VIOLATIONS.md identifies that CreateOrderUseCase directly instantiates PostgresOrderRepository, bypassing interface abstraction

checklist.name: Entity fix: remove decorators and persist logic
checklist.description: VIOLATIONS.md explains that Order should be a plain TypeScript class with no TypeORM imports; the repository adapter handles mapping

checklist.name: Use case fix: depend on interface not implementation
checklist.description: VIOLATIONS.md explains the use case should receive IOrderRepository via constructor injection, not instantiate PostgresOrderRepository directly

checklist.name: Inward dependency rule stated
checklist.description: VIOLATIONS.md states the general principle (dependencies point inward; inner layers must not import outer layers)
